---
title: "Meta Data"
execute: 
  warning: false
  message: false
fig-pos: H
format:
  pdf:
    colorlinks: true
    pdf-engine: xelatex
---

# Data

## Functions

### Latitude and Longitude


```{r}
# Assign coordinates to counties

# convert latitude/longitude points into sf objects, then join them to county polygons
library(sf)
library(dplyr)
library(tigris)
library(readr)
library(dplyr)
library(here)

options(tigris_use_cache = TRUE)

# Get US county boundaries
counties_sf <- counties(cb = TRUE, year = 2023) %>%
  st_transform(4326) %>%
  select(
    county_state = STATE_NAME,
    county_name = NAME,
    county_geoid = GEOID,
    geometry
  )


add_county_from_coords <- function(df, lat_col, lon_col, prefix = "") {
  
  points_sf <- df %>%
    filter(!is.na(.data[[lat_col]]), !is.na(.data[[lon_col]])) %>%
    st_as_sf(
      coords = c(lon_col, lat_col),
      crs = 4326,
      remove = FALSE
    )
  
  joined <- st_join(points_sf, counties_sf, join = st_within)
  
  joined %>%
    st_drop_geometry() %>%
    rename(
      "{prefix}county_state" := county_state,
      "{prefix}county_name" := county_name,
      "{prefix}county_geoid" := county_geoid
    )
}
```

### Aggregate CSVs
```{r}
aggregate_csvs <- function(path_parts,
                           output_name = "aggregated.csv",
                           dedupe = TRUE,
                           lat_col = NULL,
                           lon_col = NULL,
                           prefix = "") {
  
  data_dir <- do.call(here::here, as.list(path_parts))
  
  cat("Reading from:", data_dir, "\n")
  
  # Stop execution if the directory does not exist
  if (!dir.exists(data_dir)) {
    stop("Directory does not exist: ", data_dir)
  }
  
  # Get all CSV files in the directory
  csv_files <- list.files(
    path = data_dir,
    pattern = "\\.csv$",
    full.names = TRUE
  )
  
  # Stop if no CSV files are found
  if (length(csv_files) == 0) {
    stop("No CSV files found in: ", data_dir)
  }
  
  cat("Found", length(csv_files), "files\n")
  
  # Read each CSV file and add a column indicating its source file
  # Then combine all files into a single dataframe
  combined_data <- lapply(csv_files, function(f) {
    read_csv(f, show_col_types = FALSE) %>%
      mutate(source_file = basename(f))
  }) %>%
    bind_rows()
  
  # Remove exact duplicate rows
  if (dedupe) {
    combined_data <- combined_data %>% distinct()
  }
  
  # If latitude and longitude columns are provided, assign each row to a county using spatial join
  if (!is.null(lat_col) && !is.null(lon_col)) {
    cat("Adding county identifiers...\n")
    
    combined_data <- add_county_from_coords(
      df = combined_data,
      lat_col = lat_col,
      lon_col = lon_col,
      prefix = prefix   # allows naming like start_county_*, end_county_*
    )
  }
  
  # Extract date and time from file names for data sets that don't contain them
  combined_data <- combined_data %>%
  mutate(
    ds = as.Date(sub(".*_(\\d{4}-\\d{2}-\\d{2})_.*", "\\1", source_file)),
    hour = sub(".*_(\\d{4}-\\d{2}-\\d{2})_(\\d{4}).*", "\\2", source_file)
  )
  
  # Define output directory at the project root (outputs/)
  output_dir <- here::here("outputs")
  
  # Create the directory if it doesn't already exist
  if (!dir.exists(output_dir)) {
    dir.create(output_dir, recursive = TRUE)
  }
  
  # Build full output file path
  output_file <- file.path(output_dir, output_name)
  
  # Write the combined dataset to CSV
  write_csv(combined_data, output_file)
  
  # Print basic logging info
  cat("Saved to:", output_file, "\n")
  cat("Rows:", nrow(combined_data), "\n")
  
  # Return the final dataframe to the environment
  return(combined_data)
}
```

### Summary Statistics
```{r}
analyze_dataset <- function(df) {
  list(
    missing = df %>%
      summarise(across(everything(), ~mean(is.na(.)))),

    numeric_summary = df %>%
      summarise(across(where(is.numeric),
                       list(mean = mean, sd = sd),
                       na.rm = TRUE)),

    n_rows = nrow(df),
    n_cols = ncol(df)
  )
}
```
## General
### Business Activity

```{r}
ba_data <- aggregate_csvs(
  path_parts = c("Fern_General", "Business_Activity"),
  output_name = "business_activity_aggregated.csv",
  lat_col = "latitude",
  lon_col = "longitude",
  prefix = "")
```

### DO NOT RUN - Movement Between Places (Experiencing runtime issues)

```{r, execute=FALSE}
mp_data <- aggregate_csvs(
  path_parts = c("Fern_General", "Movement_between_Places"),
  output_name = "movement_between_places_aggregated.csv"
)

# Origin
mp_data<- add_county_from_coords(
  df = mp_data,
  lat_col = "start_latitude",
  lon_col = "start_longitude",
  prefix = "start_"
)

# Destination
mp_data <- add_county_from_coords(
  df = mp_data,
  lat_col = "end_latitude",
  lon_col = "end_longitude",
  prefix = "end_"
)

```

### Summary Statistics

```{r}
analyze_dataset(ba_data)
analyze_dataset(mp_data)
```

## Tennessee_Kentucky

### Movement Between Places During a Crisis

```{r}
tk_mp_data_admin <- aggregate_csvs(
  path_parts = c("Tennessee_Kentucky", "Fern_Movement_Crisis","Administrative_Regions"),
  output_name = "tk_movement_between_places_aggregated_admin.csv"
)
# Administrative Regions
# Origin counties
tk_mp_data_admin <- add_county_from_coords(
  df = tk_mp_data_admin,
  lat_col = "start_latitude",
  lon_col = "start_longitude",
  prefix = "start_"
)

# Destination counties
tk_mp_data_admin <- add_county_from_coords(
  df = tk_mp_data_admin,
  lat_col = "end_latitude",
  lon_col = "end_longitude",
  prefix = "end_"
)

```

```{r}
tk_mp_data_bing <- aggregate_csvs(
  path_parts = c("Tennessee_Kentucky", "Fern_Movement_Crisis","Bing_Tiles"),
  output_name = "tk_movement_between_places_aggregated_bing.csv"
)

# Bing Tiles
# Origin counties
tk_mp_data_bing <- add_county_from_coords(
  df = tk_mp_data_bing,
  lat_col = "start_latitude",
  lon_col = "start_longitude",
  prefix = "start_"
)

# Destination counties
tk_mp_data_bing <- add_county_from_coords(
  df = tk_mp_data_bing,
  lat_col = "end_latitude",
  lon_col = "end_longitude",
  prefix = "end_"
)
```

### Facebook Population during crisis

```{r}
tk_fb_data_admin <- aggregate_csvs(
  path_parts = c("Tennessee_Kentucky", "Fern_Facebook_Pop_Crisis","Administrative_Regions"),
  output_name = "tk_facebook_pop_aggregated_admin.csv",
  lat_col = "latitude",
  lon_col = "longitude",
  prefix = ""
)
```

```{r}
tk_fb_data_bing <- aggregate_csvs(
  path_parts = c("Tennessee_Kentucky", "Fern_Facebook_Pop_Crisis","Bing_Tiles"),
  output_name = "tk_facebook_pop_aggregated_bing.csv",
  lat_col = "latitude",
  lon_col = "longitude",
  prefix = ""
)
```

### Network Coverage

```{r}
tk_nc_data_active <- aggregate_csvs(
  path_parts = c("Tennessee_Kentucky", "Fern_Network_Coverage","Active"),
  output_name = "tk_network_coverage_aggregated_active.csv",
  lat_col = "lat",
  lon_col = "lon",
  prefix = ""
)
```

```{r}
tk_nc_data_undetected <- aggregate_csvs(
  path_parts = c("Tennessee_Kentucky", "Fern_Network_Coverage","Undetected"),
  output_name = "tk_network_coverage_aggregated_undetected.csv",
  lat_col = "lat",
  lon_col = "lon",
  prefix = ""
)
```


# Visualizations

Based on LA Fire [Situation Report](https://www.crisisready.io/wp-content/uploads/2025/01/ReadyMapper-LAFires-01152025.pdf)

## Maps

```{r}
library(ggplot2)
library(gt)
library(scales)
library(lubridate)

# Need to update so date can be chosen not just latest date; Check if i can do default arguments in r like in Python
make_percent_change_map <- function(file_name,
                                    date_col = "ds",
                                    value_col = "percent_change",
                                    output_plot_name = NULL,
                                    title_prefix = "Population Density Change on") {
  
  # Read file from root outputs folder
  df <- read_csv(
    here("outputs", file_name),
    show_col_types = FALSE
  )
  
  # Find latest date
  latest_date <- max(df[[date_col]], na.rm = TRUE)
  
  # Summarize to county level
  latest_county <- df %>%
    filter(.data[[date_col]] == latest_date) %>%
    group_by(county_geoid, county_name, county_state) %>%
    summarise(
      mean_percent_change = mean(.data[[value_col]], na.rm = TRUE),
      .groups = "drop"
    )
  
  # Join to county geometries
  map_data <- counties_sf %>%
    inner_join(latest_county, by = "county_geoid")
  
  # Build map
  p <- ggplot(map_data) +
    geom_sf(aes(fill = mean_percent_change), linewidth = 0.2) +
    scale_fill_gradient2(
      labels = label_number(suffix = "%", accuracy = 1)
    ) +
    labs(
      title = paste(title_prefix, latest_date),
      fill = "% Change vs Baseline"
    ) +
    theme_minimal()
  
  # Save plot if requested
  if (!is.null(output_plot_name)) {
    ggsave(
      filename = here("outputs", output_plot_name),
      plot = p,
      width = 10,
      height = 7
    )
  }
  
  return(p)
}
```


```{r}
# Not working because of earlier runtime error do not delete
make_percent_change_map(
  file_name = "movement_between_places_aggregated.csv"
)
```

```{r}
make_percent_change_map(
  file_name = "tk_facebook_pop_aggregated_bing.csv"
)
```

```{r}
make_percent_change_map(
  file_name = "tk_facebook_pop_aggregated_admin.csv"
)
```

```{r}
# Not working because of earlier runtime error do not delete
make_percent_change_map(
  file_name = "tk_movement_between_places_aggregated_admin.csv"
)
```

```{r}
# Not working because of earlier runtime error do not delete
make_percent_change_map(
  file_name = "tk_movement_between_places_aggregated_bing.csv"
)
```


## Time series

```{r}
top_counties <- tk_fb_data_admin %>%
  group_by(county_geoid, county_name) %>%
  summarise(
    max_abs_change = max(abs(percent_change), na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  slice_max(max_abs_change, n = 20) %>%
  pull(county_geoid)

fb_time <- tk_fb_data_admin %>%
  filter(county_geoid %in% top_counties) %>%
  mutate(ds = as.Date(ds)) %>%
  group_by(ds, county_geoid, county_name) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_percent_change = mean(percent_change, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

ggplot(fb_time, aes(x = ds, y = mean_percent_change)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linewidth = 0.3) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.6) +
  geom_point(size = 1) +
  facet_wrap(~ county_name, scales = "free_y") +
  scale_y_continuous() +
  labs(
    title = "% Population Density Change Over Time",
    x = NULL,
    y = "% Change vs Baseline"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
```

```{r}
network_time <- tk_nc_data_active %>%
  mutate(ds = as.Date(ds)) %>%
  group_by(ds, county_name) %>%
  summarise(
    pct_coverage = mean(coverage, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

ggplot(network_time, aes(x = ds, y = pct_coverage)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.6) +
  geom_point(size = 1) +
  facet_wrap(~ county_name) +
  scale_y_continuous() +
  labs(
    title = "Network Coverage Over Time",
    x = NULL,
    y = "Coverage"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
```


```{r}
county_flows <- tk_mp_data_admin %>%
  group_by(start_county_name, end_county_name) %>%
  summarise(
    total_crisis_movement = sum(n_crisis, na.rm = TRUE),
    avg_z_score = mean(z_score, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  arrange(desc(total_crisis_movement))

county_flows %>%
  slice_tail(n = 20) %>%
  gt() %>%
  tab_header(
    title = "Bottom County-to-County Movement Flows"
  )

# Top 20 wasn't very interesting. Bottom 20 says something more informative 
```
